tossups.question: The narrator of this novel contrasts those in life with a “round-trip ticket” with himself, who sings the “One-Way Ticket Blues.” Upon reaching a fire tower, this novel’s narrator repeats to himself the title character’s wishes of a “Radio and mirror,” noting that they both “connect one person with another.” Because his union and private lives intersect perfectly, a coworker in this novel is called “Möbius man.” A crow-trap nicknamed “Hope” is found to collect water by this novel’s protagonist, who is goaded into sex with the title character by villagers of a remote seaside town. Near this novel’s opening, a rope ladder vanishes from a pit where an amateur entomologist is collecting insects, resigning him to a life of shoveling sand. For 10 points, Niki Jumpei (“joom-pay”) gets trapped in the desert with the title widow of what novel by Kobo Abe (“AH-bay”)?
tossups.question_sanitized: The narrator of this novel contrasts those in life with a "round-trip ticket" with himself, who sings the "One-Way Ticket Blues." Upon reaching a fire tower, this novel's narrator repeats to himself the title character's wishes of a "Radio and mirror," noting that they both "connect one person with another." Because his union and private lives intersect perfectly, a coworker in this novel is called "Mobius man." A crow-trap nicknamed "Hope" is found to collect water by this novel's protagonist, who is goaded into sex with the title character by villagers of a remote seaside town. Near this novel's opening, a rope ladder vanishes from a pit where an amateur entomologist is collecting insects, resigning him to a life of shoveling sand. For 10 points, Niki Jumpei ("joom-pay") gets trapped in the desert with the title widow of what novel by Kobo Abe ("AH-bay")?
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Woman in the Dunes [or Suna no Onna]

packet.name: I. Prelims Tiebreaker - LSE, McGill, UNC A, Virginia, WUSTL A

bonuses.leadin: This author wrote the play The Man Who Turned into a Stick. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this author who created a character whose personality changes dramatically after his face is burned and he makes a new plastic mask for himself in The Face of Another.
Kobo Abe is most famous for this novel, in which the entomologist Niki Junpei becomes trapped with a stranger in a village digging sand every day.
Abe wrote a novel about a "ruined" one of these. Jim Hawkins acquires one of these from Billy Bones near the beginning of Treasure Island.
answers: The Woman in the Dunes [or Suna no onna]
answers_sanitized: The Woman in the Dunes [or Suna no onna]
